text:
Question: What is a deductible?
Category: Cost & Payment
Subcategory: Deductibles
Internal Answer: A deductible is the specific dollar amount you must pay out-of-pocket for covered medical expenses before your insurance plan begins to pay benefits. It typically resets annually. Different plans have different deductible amounts, ranging from very low to high depending on the plan design.
Client Answer: A deductible is the amount you pay out-of-pocket for covered healthcare services before your insurance starts to pay. For example, if your deductible is AED 500, you'll pay the first AED 500 of covered services yourself, and then your insurance will start covering costs according to your plan. Most plans have an annual deductible.
Priority: High
Status: Approved

Rule: Waiting Period Application
Category: Eligibility & Underwriting
Description: Waiting periods are mandatory time intervals between policy effective date and when certain benefits become available. Common waiting periods include: 30 days for general treatments, 90 days for specific conditions, 12 months for maternity benefits. Pre-existing conditions may have longer waiting periods (often 12-24 months) or may be excluded. Waiting periods ensure new policyholders don't immediately claim large benefits for existing conditions.
Internal Usage Note: Always check plan-specific waiting period schedules. Waiting periods can be waived in group plans with prior coverage continuity. Critical illness waiting periods are typically 90 days minimum.
Client-Safe Summary: Some health insurance plans have waiting periods before certain benefits become available. A waiting period is a set amount of time after your coverage starts during which specific services may not be covered. Different services may have different waiting periods. Check your policy documents for details on waiting periods that apply to your plan.
Status: Active

Mapping: FAQ-005 to Related Rules and Plans

Question: Does my plan cover dental? (FAQ-005)
Primary Rule: RULE-003 (Benefit Categories and Coverage Types)
Related Plan: PLAN-003 (Basic Family Plan - PLACEHOLDER)
Confidence: High
Answer Type: Requires Document Reference

This FAQ requires plan-specific confirmation. The primary rule explains general benefit categories, but actual dental coverage varies by plan. Direct customer to check policy documents for specific dental benefits.

Status: Approved
Needs Plan Link: Yes
Needs Rule Link: Yes
Needs Network Check: No

Question: How do I refill my prescriptions?
Category: Claims & Benefits Usage
Subcategory: Prescription Drugs
Internal Answer: Prescription refill process depends on the pharmacy provider, insurance plan, and whether it's a mail-order or retail refill. Typically members can refill at in-network pharmacies, use pharmacy apps/websites, or use mail-order for 90-day supplies. Our specific operational refill process has not yet been finalized.
Client Answer: To refill prescriptions, you'll typically need to contact your pharmacy directly or use their online/app refill system if available. For specific instructions on the refill process covered by your plan, including any mail-order pharmacy options, please contact customer service for detailed guidance.
Priority: Medium
Status: Pending Detail

Rule: Pre-existing Condition Non-Disclosure Review
Category: Eligibility & Underwriting
Description: Providing accurate health information at enrollment is important for proper coverage determination. If undisclosed pre-existing conditions are discovered after policy issuance, the insurer may review the policy terms. This could affect coverage for related conditions. We encourage members to provide complete health information during enrollment to ensure appropriate coverage.
Internal Usage Note: This rule was softened from harsh original language. Original stated significant penalties, but current version emphasizes importance of disclosure without threatening language. Requires compliance and legal review before use in customer communications.
Client-Safe Summary: When enrolling, please provide accurate information about your health history. This ensures you receive appropriate coverage for your needs.
Status: Needs Review

Plan: Verified Basic Individual Plan (Real Product Data)

Plan ID: PLAN-101
Plan Name: Basic Individual Health Plan
Plan Type: Individual
Monthly Premium: AED 450
Annual Deductible: AED 500
Outpatient Copay: AED 50 per visit
Inpatient Coinsurance: 20% after deductible
Out-of-Pocket Maximum: AED 10,000 per year

Coverage Includes:
- Annual health screening
- Outpatient consultations (after copay)
- Emergency care (100% coverage after deductible)
- Inpatient hospitalization (80% coverage after deductible)
- Diagnostic tests and lab work
- Prescription drugs (Formulary Tier 1 and 2, subject to copay)

Exclusions:
- Dental and vision care (available as riders)
- Alternative medicine
- Cosmetic procedures

Effective Date: 2026-01-01
Status: Active
coverage_summary: Annual screening, outpatient, emergency, inpatient, diagnostics, prescriptions
